title: Dashboard E3 — History replay time-travel rule state
description: Implement time-travel rule state replay using .intent-ledger.jsonl rehydration. Add GET /api/replay?at=<isoTimestamp> endpoint that reconstructs the rule state (AgentsMeta + active human-locks) as of the specified timestamp by replaying all ledger entries up to that point. Add a HistoryReplay view in the Preact SPA with a timeline scrubber; moving the scrubber re-renders the RulesTree and HumanLockVault views with the historical state. Depends on TASK-009 (SPA scaffold).

files.change: New Express router: GET /api/replay?at=ISO8601; reads .intent-ledger.jsonl; filters entries where entry.ts <= at; reconstructs rule state snapshot: {rules: AgentsMeta, humanLock: HumanLockEntry[], ledger: LedgerEntry[]}; returns snapshot JSON; 400 if at parameter missing or invalid

files.target: HistoryReplay view — timeline scrubber + historical state views
files.change: New Preact view: fetches GET /api/ledger to get all timestamps; renders timeline scrubber (date range slider); on scrubber change: fetches GET /api/replay?at={selectedTimestamp}; renders RulesTree and HumanLockVault components with historical data (not live data); shows 'Viewing state as of {timestamp}' banner; Reset button returns to live view
Add 5th nav tab '#/history' → HistoryReplay view; update hash router
Add getReplay(at: string): Promise<ReplaySnapshot> fetch function
Import replayRouter from api/replay.ts; app.use('/api', replayRouter)

implementation: Implement api/replay.ts: rehydration function replayToTimestamp(projectRoot, at): read all ledger entries from .intent-ledger.jsonl; filter by ts <= at; reconstruct AgentsMeta state by applying AI ledger entries sequentially (rule changes); reconstruct HumanLockEntry[] active locks at that timestamp

Note: full AgentsMeta rehydration requires ledger entries to capture rule state changes — if the current ledger format doesn't capture this, replay returns only ledger entries up to timestamp (partial replay)
Implement HistoryReplay Preact view with range slider input; derive timestamps from ledger entries
Add to app.tsx navigation and api/client.ts

reference.pattern: Event sourcing / replay pattern on JSONL log
reference.examples: Ledger endpoint from TASK-007; RulesTree and HumanLockVault views from TASK-009 reused with injected historical data

criteria: GET /api/replay with invalid or missing at parameter returns HTTP 400
Dashboard History tab renders timeline scrubber with timestamps from ledger
Moving the scrubber to a past timestamp re-renders views with historical data
Reset button returns to live view

rationale.chosen_approach: Rehydrate from .intent-ledger.jsonl as append-only event log; JSONL is the natural source of truth for time-ordered state reconstruction
alternatives_considered: Periodic snapshots (requires additional storage and snapshot management)
Full git history integration (complex, requires git access in server context)
decision_factors: intent-ledger.jsonl is already append-only log with timestamps — natural event source
rationale.tradeoffs: Replay quality depends on how completely ledger entries capture state changes; initial version may show only partial state (ledger entries, not full AgentsMeta diff). Noted in implementation note.

manual_checks: Generate some intent-ledger entries across different timestamps, then GET /api/replay?at=<past_timestamp> — verify returned ledger only contains entries before that time
Navigate to History tab — verify scrubber appears with timestamp range
Move scrubber to past time — verify views show historical data
success_metrics: Scrubber interaction triggers re-fetch and view update

risks.description: Ledger entries don't capture enough state to fully reconstruct AgentsMeta at a past timestamp
risks.mitigation: For v1 replay, return only ledger entries up to timestamp (partial view); document full state replay as v1.2 enhancement requiring richer ledger schema
risks.fallback: Ship as 'ledger history viewer' (time-filtered ledger entries) which is still valuable; label full state replay as future work
